questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the option that correctly completes the sentence: 'By the time the rescue team arrived, the trapped miners ____ for nearly ten hours.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An action continuing up to a point in the past uses the past perfect continuous tense.

**Working.** The reference point ('the team arrived') is in the past, and the waiting was ongoing before it. This requires 'had been + verb-ing'.

**Answer.** had been waiting.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert $72$ km/h into m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply km/h by $\tfrac{5}{18}$ to get m/s.

**Working.** $72\times\dfrac{5}{18}=20$ m/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m/s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The least common multiple (LCM) of $6$ and $8$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** LCM is the product of all prime factors at highest powers.

**Working.** $6=2\cdot 3$, $8=2^3$; LCM $=2^3\cdot 3=24$.

**Answer.** $24$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the word most similar in meaning to 'ABATE'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Abate' means to become less intense or to reduce.

**Working.** Among the options, 'diminish' is the closest synonym; 'increase' is its antonym.

**Answer.** diminish.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: What is $35\%$ of $240$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Percent means parts per hundred.

**Working.**
$$\frac{35}{100}\times 240 = 84$$

**Answer.** $84$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Notice: 'During a fire, use the stairs; do not use the lift.' The assumption underlying this notice is that
options: lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire
there is never a real fire
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An assumption is what must be taken for granted for the advice to make sense.

**Working.** Advising against lifts during a fire presumes lifts are unsafe then.

**Answer.** lifts may malfunction or be unsafe during a fire.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: X is the brother of the son of Y's son. How is X related to Y?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Resolve generations from the inside out.

**Working.** 'The son of Y's son' is Y's grandson; X is his brother, hence also Y's grandson.

**Answer.** Grandson.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A boat's speed in still water is $10$ km/h and the stream flows at $2$ km/h. The time taken to travel $24$ km downstream is _____ hours.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed = boat + stream.

**Working.** Downstream speed $=10+2=12$ km/h; time $=\dfrac{24}{12}=2$ h.

**Answer.** $2$ h.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next letter in the series: A, C, E, G, ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The letters advance by two positions each time (odd-positioned letters).

**Working.** After G (7th letter), skip H to reach I.

**Answer.** I.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two triangles are similar with corresponding sides in the ratio $2:3$. The ratio of their areas is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Areas of similar figures scale as the square of the side ratio.

**Working.** $(2:3)^2 = 4:9$.

**Answer.** $4:9$.

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: For the rectangular settling tank shown, read the flow $Q$ (in MLD) and the plan area $A$ from the diagram. The surface overflow rate $Q/A$ is _____ m³/m²/day.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="40" width="250" height="90" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.08)" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M20 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="14" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">Q = 8 MLD</text><path d="M290 60 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="300" y="55" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">out</text><line x1="40" y1="48" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="120" cy="80" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="170" cy="100" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><circle cx="210" cy="118" r="2" fill="#1e293b"/><path d="M120 86 L132 124 M170 106 L182 126" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="0.8"/><text x="120" y="150" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">plan area A = 400 m²</text><text x="160" y="36" font-size="8" fill="#2563eb">surface (overflow)</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $SOR=\dfrac{Q}{A}$ (1 MLD = 1000 m³/day), with $Q$ and $A$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{8\times1000}{400}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m/day}$

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">6</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$6+1.5=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: Which are recognised biodiversity hotspots in/around India?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Western Ghats, Himalaya and Indo-Burma are global hotspots; the Thar is not (low endemism/richness threshold).

**Working.**
First three.

**Answer.** Western Ghats, Himalaya, Indo-Burma

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Waste of mass $1000\,\text{kg}$ at loose density $100\,\text{kg/m}^3$ is compacted to $500\,\text{kg/m}^3$. The volume reduction is _____ %.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $VR=\left(1-\dfrac{V_2}{V_1}\right)\times100=\left(1-\dfrac{\rho_1}{\rho_2}\right)\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{100}{500}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Wet MSW $1500\,\text{kg}$ at $40\%$ moisture. The dry-solids mass is _____ kg.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dry = wet × (1 − moisture).

**Working.**
$$1500(1-40/100)=900.$$

**Answer.** $900\,\text{kg}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A field device estimates wastewater BOD within minutes by measuring the respiration of immobilised microbes through a dissolved-oxygen electrode. The biological recognition element and the transducer are, respectively,
options: immobilised microorganisms and the dissolved-oxygen electrode
the DO electrode and the microbes
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a microbial BOD biosensor the immobilised microbes are the bio-recognition element; the O₂ they consume is sensed by the DO electrode (the transducer).

**Working.**
Recognition = microbes; transducer = DO electrode.

**Answer.** Microbes; DO electrode

questions.solution:
**Concept.** L = L₁ + 10log n.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log4=86.02.$$

**Answer.** $86.02\,\text{dB}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: Drinking-water standards require E. coli to be ABSENT in any 100 mL sample. A membrane-filtration test of a 100 mL sample of treated water yields 8 E. coli colonies. The correct interpretation is that the water
options: fails the potability standard and is unsafe to drink
is potable, since 8 is a low count
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The standard is zero E. coli per 100 mL; 8 colonies/100 mL exceeds it, signalling recent faecal contamination — the water is unsafe.

**Working.**
Compare the measured count to the zero-tolerance standard.

**Answer.** Fails / unsafe

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: The cyclical framework underpinning an ISO 14001 EMS is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** ISO 14001 follows the PDCA continual-improvement loop.

**Working.**
PDCA.

**Answer.** Plan-Do-Check-Act

$$\dfrac{6\times1000}{400}=15.$$

**Answer.** $15$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{400}\right)\times100=80\%.$$

**Answer.** $80\%$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Ammonia-bearing water is chlorinated. From the breakpoint-chlorination curve shown, read the breakpoint dose. To leave a free residual of $1.5\,\text{mg/L}$, the chlorine that must be applied is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="38" y1="20" x2="38" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="38" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="4" y="30" font-size="8" fill="#1e293b">residual</text><text x="232" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">Cl₂ dose (mg/L) →</text><path d="M38 165 L95 120 L150 150 L205 160 L300 80" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.7"/><line x1="205" y1="55" x2="205" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="205" cy="160" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="170" y="50" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">breakpoint</text><text x="186" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">8</text><text x="250" y="92" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">free residual</text></svg>
figure.caption: Breakpoint dose marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Beyond the breakpoint, extra chlorine appears as free residual: applied = breakpoint dose + desired free residual.

**Working.**
$$8+1.5=9.5.$$

**Answer.** $9.5\,\text{mg/L}$

questions.solution:

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $5\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$ of flue gas. Reading the collecting area $A$ and the drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its Deutsch–Anderson collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.1 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Deutsch–Anderson: $\eta=1-e^{-wA/Q}$, with $A$ and $w$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-e^{-0.1\times200/5}\right)\times100=98.17\%.$$

**Answer.** $98.17\%$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=120\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 50 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 6 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 25 m,  σ_z = 25 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120}{\pi\times6\times25\times25}e^{-50^2/(2\times25^2)}=0.00137851.$$

**Answer.** $13.785\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: Compared with a slow sand filter, a rapid sand filter is characterised by
options: higher filtration rate and reliance on prior coagulation

Higher rate, needs coagulation.

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.3</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.3\times40}{50+40}=0.133.$$

**Answer.** $0.133$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 60</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times100}{60+100}=0.313.$$

**Answer.** $0.313$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: A pollutant decays first-order with $k=0.3\,\text{d}^{-1}$. Reading the initial concentration $C_0$ from the figure, its concentration after $7$ d is _____ mg/L.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 170" width="100%" style="max-width:300px;height:auto"><rect x="120" y="75" width="60" height="75" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.25" stroke="#1e293b"/><rect x="120" y="30" width="60" height="120" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t = 0</text><text x="150" y="96" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">C₀ = 150</text><text x="150" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">mg/L</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial concentration C₀ marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$150e^{-0.3\times7}=18.37.$$

**Answer.** $18.37$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.02\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $10$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 1000</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Initial population N₀ marked on the curve at t = 0. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$1000e^{0.02\times10}=1221.$$

**Answer.** $1221$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $10\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 4</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.1)^{4}}=81962.$$

**Answer.** $₹81962$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Doubling atmospheric CO₂ adds a radiative forcing of $3.7\,\text{W/m}^2$. For a climate-sensitivity parameter of $0.8\,\text{K per W/m}^2$, the equilibrium surface warming is _____ K.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equilibrium warming $\Delta T=\lambda\,\Delta F$ (climate sensitivity × forcing) — GHGs absorb the outgoing long-wave IR.

**Working.**
$$0.8\times3.7=2.96\,\text{K}.$$

**Answer.** $2.96\,\text{K}$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A diesel-contaminated soil is cleaned in place by injecting air and nutrients to stimulate indigenous hydrocarbon-degrading bacteria, with no excavation. This technique is best classified as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Treating soil where it lies by stimulating native microbes with O₂/nutrients is in-situ bioremediation (bioventing); ex-situ methods require excavation, and incineration/S-S are not biological.

**Working.**
No excavation + stimulate native microbes ⇒ in-situ.

**Answer.** In-situ bioremediation

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 188" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="36" y1="150" x2="304" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="60" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="83" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">50</text><text x="83" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CO₂</text><rect x="142" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="165" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">2</text><text x="165" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">CH₄</text><rect x="224" y="144" width="46" height="6" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="247" y="140" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">1</text><text x="247" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">N₂O</text><text x="170" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$50+2\times28+1\times265=371.$$

**Answer.** $371$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Modified Dulong $HHV=337C+1419(H-O/8)$ kJ/kg with $C=40\%$, $H=6\%$, $O=42\%$. HHV is _____ kJ/kg.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="70" y="30" width="46" height="120" fill="#dc2626" opacity="0.75"/><text x="93" y="26" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">in 14.5</text><rect x="190" y="146" width="46" height="4" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.8"/><text x="213" y="142" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">out 0</text><text x="160" y="170" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">HHV (MJ/kg)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Energy content of the waste fuel.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Apply the modified Dulong formula.

**Working.**
$$337(40)+1419(6-42/8)=14544.$$

**Answer.** $14544\,\text{kJ/kg}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: A discrete particle ($\rho_s=2650\,\text{kg/m}^3$) settles in water ($\rho=1000$, $\mu=10^{-3}\,\text{Pa·s}$). Reading its diameter from the figure, its terminal velocity is _____ ×10⁻³ m/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 180" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><rect x="60" y="20" width="180" height="140" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.06)" stroke="#1e293b"/><circle cx="150" cy="66" r="12" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><line x1="138" y1="66" x2="162" y2="66" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1"/><text x="168" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">d = 0.02 mm</text><path d="M150 82 l0 52 m-4 -6 l4 6 4 -6" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="158" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">v_s</text><text x="150" y="174" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">ρ_s = 2650 kg/m³, water μ = 10⁻³ Pa·s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle diameter marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $v_s=\dfrac{g(\rho_s-\rho)d^2}{18\mu}$, with $d$ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{9.81\times1650\times(0.00002)^2}{18\times10^{-3}}=0.00036\,\text{m/s}.$$

**Answer.** $0.36\times10^{-3}$

questions.subject: Solid & Hazardous Waste Management
questions.stem: Read the loose and compacted densities from the bar chart. The volume reduction $(1-\rho_1/\rho_2)$ achieved is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 188" width="100%" style="max-width:320px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="150" x2="280" y2="150" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="80" y="134" width="50" height="16" fill="#2563eb" opacity="0.8"/><text x="105" y="130" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">80</text><text x="105" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">loose ρ₁</text><rect x="180" y="30" width="50" height="120" fill="#16a34a" opacity="0.85"/><text x="205" y="26" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">600</text><text x="205" y="164" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">compacted ρ₂</text><text x="160" y="182" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">kg/m³</text></svg>
figure.caption: Loose and compacted densities shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** VR = (1 − ρ₁/ρ₂)×100, both densities read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$\left(1-\dfrac{80}{600}\right)\times100=86.7\%.$$

**Answer.** $86.7\%$
